text:
Once upon a time, there was a humble woodcutter who loved giant sequoias. Every dawn, he'd travel deep into the forest, armed only with his ax. Deep within these woods, a giant sequoia tree stood towering majestically. The woodcutter adored it.

One day, while clearing some underbrush away, he stumbled upon something precious—a tiny seed from the tree. Could this tiny seed grow into something incredible?

With pride, the woodcutter returned home. He dug a little hole outside his cabin, buried the seed, and watered it everyday. Days turned into weeks. The seed sprouted into a small stalk. Soon enough, a seedpod fell off, releasing a tiny seed.

The woodcutter saved every seed. They planted them everywhere; fields, forests, even on top of mountains. Years passed. He became famous. There wasnhips everywhere.

Years later, the great tree grew back. It searched for the seed it had
